pullRequest.title: Silence PDBConstructionWarning in test_PDB_StructureAlignment
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Wrap PDB structure parsing calls with `warnings.catch_warnings()` to suppress `PDBConstructionWarning` messages during test execution in `test_PDB_StructureAlignment.py`.

## Changes

- Added `import warnings` to the imports
- Added `from Bio.PDB.PDBExceptions import PDBConstructionWarning` import
- Wrapped all `p.get_structure()` calls with warning suppression context manager

This follows the same pattern used in other test files:
- `test_PDB_MMCIFParser.py`
- `test_mmtf_online.py`
- `test_RCSBFormats.py`

## Testing

All 4 tests in `test_PDB_StructureAlignment.py` pass:
```
test_PDB_StructureAlignment.py::StructureAlignTests::test_StructAlign PASSED
test_PDB_StructureAlignment.py::StructureAlignTests::test_automatic_alignment_generation PASSED
test_PDB_StructureAlignment.py::StructureAlignTests::test_custom_vs_automatic_alignment PASSED
test_PDB_StructureAlignment.py::StructureAlignTests::test_msa_vs_alignment_objects PASSED
```

Fixes #1877

---

I agree to license my contributions under both the Biopython License Agreement and the BSD 3-Clause License.

pullRequest.title: Run PSEA in a temporary directory
pullRequest.body:
~This PR updates Bio.PDB.PSEA.run_psea() to execute the external
PSEA tool inside a temporary directory, This prevents unnecessary
output files from being left in the user's working directory.

Fixes #4031

Closes #4768 

Since the "Analyzing structures" part of the documentation was getting crowded, I made a new section named "Superimposing two structures". I moved the previous documentation for using the Superimposer class and for "Mapping the residues of two related structures onto each other" into this new section, and added documentation for using CEAlign and QCPSuperimposer. I also added the citations in the docstrings of each of these classes to the bibliography.

pullRequest.title: Aligner default arguments
pullRequest.body:
To be consistent with `Bio.pairwise2`, the pairwise aligner in `Bio.Align` uses a match score of +1, a mismatch score of 0, and a gap score of 0 by default.
However, this is almost never a good scoring scheme. In particular, any mismatch can be replaced by an insertion followed by a deletion, or a deletion followed by an insertion, and yield the same score.
This results in a large number of alignments with only trivial differences between them.
For example, aligning two sequences of 75 randomly chosen letters against each other yields >9223372036854775807 alignments (i.e., greater than sys.maxsize).
In contrast, with gap_score = -1 only one or a few alignments are returned.

To alert users to this change, a warning is issued the first time an aligner is used in which the gap score was not set explicitly.

This PR does not change the default parameters for `Bio.pairwise2`, as that module is already deprecated.

pullRequest.body:

<!--- Briefly describe the changes included in this pull request below
 !--- starting with 'Closes #...' if appropriate --->

Closes #5004 

- The Newton-Raphson method within the QCP algorithm stopped early if value of `mxEigenV` was at any point smaller than the old value. Added absolute value between the difference to fix this.
- Added two new test cases for QCPSuperimposer with points along two lines of slightly different lengths and oriented differently. One test checks that the results is the same between QCPSuperimposer and SVDSuperimposer and other checks the RMSD value from the QCP algorithm and compares it to the value calculated from the transformed coordinates. The old code fails both tests and new passes them.
- Modified the CEAligner tests since the hardcoded RMS values were now wrong. Mostly this reverted and old change, so these new ones seem to be correct. Also double checked by substituting the SVDSuperimposer into CEAligner and got the same answer.

Closes #5006 

In the following PR, I made the fasta_align parameter optional in the StructureAlignment class and also made it None by default. This means that the StructureAlignment class is still compatible with legacy code, but the new API allows to just pass in the two structure models (as either kwargs or by passing None as the first arg). Since this may be a bit confusing, I also updated the documentation, giving more context and also explaining how to use the API, and added type annotations to the __init__ of StructureAlignment to give better clues as to how it works.

Closes #5038 

This change fixes a symmetry issue with alignment using pickled version of the PairwiseAlginer - the epsilon property was missing and thus resulted in differing alignments using the unpickled object. Adds a test for a specific query that is sensitive to the issue, in addition to assertions against the algorithm property (for future proofing).

pullRequest.title: Align: add substitution matrix support to Alignment.format()
pullRequest.body:
Closes #5043

This pull request extends the Alignment.format() functionality to support substitution matrices for pretty-printing alignments.

Summary of changes
	•	Extended Alignment.format() to accept either:
	•	an optional substitution matrix, or
	•	a PairwiseAligner object (using its substitution matrix).
	•	Updated all relevant helper functions accordingly.
	•	Added a new test file: Tests/test_format_matrix.py, which verifies pretty-print output under different substitution matrices (e.g. NUC.4.4, BLASTN-like).
	•	Confirmed that all Biopython unit tests pass locally.

Notes
	•	Default behavior remains unchanged (identities = |, positive matches = :, mismatches = ., gaps = -).
	•	Verified that lowercase residues are handled case-insensitively.
	•	Contributors listing updated in both NEWS.rst and CONTRIB.rst.

pullRequest.title: SnapGene: Filter out partial binding sites.
pullRequest.body:
The "primers" packet within a SnapGene file may contain, for every primers, additional partial binding sites in addition to the full binding site.

We use the minimum values described in the `HybridizationParams` element to filter out any binding site that:

* has a matching length shorter than the minimum accepted matching length, or
* has a melting temperature lower than the minimum accepted melting temperature.

---

pullRequest.title: Update ScanProsite to handle the new XML format from the ScanProsite API
pullRequest.body: - [X] I hereby agree to dual licence this and any previous contributions under both the _Biopython License Agreement_ **AND** the _BSD 3-Clause License_. - [X] I have read the ``CONTRIBUTING.rst`` file, have run ``pre-commit`` locally, and understand that continuous integration checks will be used to confirm the Biopython unit tests and style checks pass with these changes. - [X] I have added my name to the alphabetical contributors listings in the files ``NEWS.rst`` and ``CONTRIB.rst`` as part of this pull request, am listed already, or do not wish to be listed. (*This acknowledgement is optional.*) Closes #5073 The issue was resolved by the ScanProsite API team at the Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics (SIB), and I have included my correspondence with SIB in the issue comments. While testing, it became clear that the XML response from the ScanProsite API has changed and breaks BioPython's parsing of the response. These changes are a quick fix for that. Additional considerations for the future may include: 1) Better handling of the response from ScanProsite to inform the user if an error is in the API response, or in BioPython code itself 2) Use json as the default output parameter in `ScanProsite.scan()` because I suspect the structure of the response from the ScanProsite API is less likely to change in this format 3) And, if 3, then refactor the parsing of the ScanProsite API response to handle json 4) Warn the user if they have specified unsupported keyword argument values (e.g. in the case of #5073, the user specified "uniprot" as the db value which is not supported by the ScanProsite API and results in defaulting to the Swiss-Prot ("sp") database only. @peterjc , perhaps you can let me know if you think I should take a stab at any/all of the above. ### Testing Ran script: ``` from Bio.ExPASy import ScanProsite pattern = "P-x(2)-G-E-S-G(2)-[AS]" url = "https://prosite.expasy.org/" sp_result = ScanProsite.scan(sig=pattern, mirror=url, output='xml', db='sp') tr_result = ScanProsite.scan(sig=pattern, mirror=url, output='xml', db='tr') sequences = ScanProsite.read(sp_result) + ScanProsite.read(tr_result) print(len(sequences), "sequences found") ``` Output: ``` 1089 sequences found ``` UI Output:

pullRequest.title: Migrate to pyproject.toml
pullRequest.body:
<!--- Please read each of the following items and confirm by replacing
 !--the [ ] with a [X] --->

- [X] I hereby agree to dual licence this and any previous contributions under both
the _Biopython License Agreement_ **AND** the _BSD 3-Clause License_.

- [X] I have read the ``CONTRIBUTING.rst`` file, have run ``pre-commit``
locally, and understand that continuous integration checks will be used to
confirm the Biopython unit tests and style checks pass with these changes.

- [X] I have added my name to the alphabetical contributors listings in the files
``NEWS.rst`` and ``CONTRIB.rst`` as part of this pull request, am listed
already, or do not wish to be listed. (*This acknowledgement is optional.*)

 
 # Contents for the PR

Replaces `setup.py` packaging configuration with `pyproject.toml`, including everything associated with this change: ci, documentation, etc. (hopefully I didn't miss anything)

# Potential points of discussion

## Running tests

Currently `python setup.py test --offline` produces the following files in the root directory which are not gitignored (am I doing something wrong?):

```
other_trees.nex
other_trees.xml
tree1.nwk
tree1.xml
```

Since I opted for completely deleting `setup.py` the new equivalent way to write tests would be:
```
cd Tests
python run_tests.py
```
However, In my testing I had less errors when running the code bellow compared to above (8 vs 11)?
```
python Tests/run_tests.py
```
Is there a specific reason we need to change directory to the `Tests` folder?


## Keywords

`pyproject.toml` supports project `keywords` in the `project` section. I didn't add any, but I can add them if you propose a list.

## License

As per the [docs](https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/guides/writing-pyproject-toml/#license-and-license-files), since the Biopython license is not a valid [spdx](https://spdx.org/licenses/) license, I can write
```toml
[project]
license = "LicenseRef-Biopython OR BSD-3-Clause"
``` 
I hope this is correct.

## Setuptools

New minimum version is `74.1` since it supports `[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]`

Since `setuptools` is required to run tests (because `setuptoosl.find_packages()` is used), I added `setuptools` to the 
`dev` depedency group so they can be installed via `pip install -e . --group dev`. This prevents the `ModuleNotFoundError` for `setuptools` and is more friendly with other package managers such as `uv` 

## RPM Builds

Are now more involved, since its no longer a 1-liner, but as per the [docs](https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/discussions/setup-py-deprecated/#what-commands-should-be-used-instead)
the command `python setup.py bdist_rpm` should not be used anymore. 


Potentially closes #5092

Closes #5097 

`_ATOM_FORMAT_STRING` is missing a space between the B-factor column and the final element. Therefore, `.pdb` saved with BioPython can't be read back into RDKit. This change just adds the required space which was probably accidentally removed in #5047 . Changes include a new test.

Closes #5098

Fix the error message that is raised when the target specified in `tree.trace()` is not found in the tree. The error message now includes the full target. Included a test for this.

### Testing

Given `simple_modified.dnd`
```
(((Aaa,Bbb)AB,(Ccc,Ddd)CD)AD,(Eee,Fff,Ggg)EG)AG;
```
Ran
```
from Bio import Phylo
tree = Phylo.read("..\\biopython-issues\\5098\\simple_modified.dnd", "newick")
tree.trace("AAA", "Ggg")
```
Raises:
```
ValueError: target 'AAA' is not in this tree
```
